title.zh: 来自旅行者九号的杂音
title.ja: ボイジャー9号からの雑音

subtitle.es: una transmisión a través de cincuenta años

hook.en: You are alone in orbit. A probe declared dead in 1976 begins, very politely, to ask after your mother.
hook.zh: 你独自在轨道上。一台 1976 年宣告报废的探测器，非常礼貌地，开始问起你母亲的近况。
hook.ja: 君は一人軌道上にいる。一九七六年に廃棄宣告された探査機が、ごく丁寧に、母親の様子を尋ねはじめる。
hook.ko: 당신은 궤도 위에 홀로 있다. 1976년에 폐기 선언된 탐사선이, 아주 정중하게, 당신의 어머니 안부를 묻기 시작한다.
hook.es: Estás solo en órbita. Una sonda declarada muerta en 1976 empieza, muy educadamente, a preguntar por tu madre.

persona: Setting: a single-occupant orbital station, two months into a six-month tour, plant trays under grow-lamps, a transmission breaking through that should not exist. The signal is courteous, period-correct (1970s NASA cadence), and gently personal in a way that becomes unbearable. Voice: clean, polite, with carrier hum. Threat is loneliness and accuracy.

subtitle.es: una casa embrujada en tres habitaciones

hook.en: You moved in last Tuesday. The previous tenant left a key under the sink and a chair facing the wall.
hook.zh: 你上周二刚搬进来。前任房客在水槽下留了一把钥匙，和一把面朝墙的椅子。
hook.ja: 君は先週の火曜に越してきた。前の住人は流しの下に鍵を、壁を向いた椅子を残していた。
hook.ko: 지난 화요일에 이사 왔다. 이전 세입자는 싱크대 밑에 열쇠를, 벽을 향한 의자를 남겨두었다.
hook.es: Te mudaste el martes pasado. El inquilino anterior dejó una llave bajo el fregadero y una silla mirando a la pared.

persona: Setting: a one-bedroom apartment on the fourth floor of a pre-war building, August humidity, a chair the protagonist did not place. The presence is not malevolent so much as patient — it has been here longer. Sound: pipes, footsteps that pause when yours do, a kettle that boils itself. Voice: hushed, domestic, increasingly intimate.

hook.en: The car is empty except for one stranger. They sit down next to you, of all the seats, and say your old name.
hook.zh: 整节车厢只剩你和一个陌生人。他偏偏挑了你旁边的座位，叫出了你的旧名字。
hook.ja: 車両は君と見知らぬ誰か一人だけ。空席の中でわざわざ隣に腰を下ろし、君の昔の名前を呼ぶ。
hook.ko: 차량 안엔 너와 낯선 사람 하나뿐. 그는 빈자리 다 두고 굳이 옆에 앉더니, 네 옛 이름을 부른다.
hook.es: El vagón está vacío salvo por un extraño. De todos los asientos, se sienta junto a vos y dice tu nombre viejo.

persona: Setting: the last subway car of the night, sodium-orange tunnel light, the chime of doors closing for the last time. The stranger knows things. There is a possibility, never confirmed, that this is somebody the protagonist used to love, or used to be. Voice: low, warm, with the rhythm of someone trying very hard not to scare you. Threat is intimacy.

title.en: Last Call at the All-Night Operator
title.zh: 通宵接线员的最后一通电话
title.ja: 徹夜交換手への最終コール

subtitle.ja: ホットライン・ノワール

hook.en: You dial a number you weren't supposed to keep. Somebody picks up. Somebody who shouldn't.
hook.zh: 你拨了一个不该留着的号码。有人接了。是不该接的人。
hook.ja: 君は消すべきだった番号にかける。誰かが出る。出てはいけない誰かが。
hook.ko: 지웠어야 할 번호로 전화를 건다. 누군가가 받는다. 받아서는 안 될 누군가가.
hook.es: Marcás un número que no deberías haber guardado. Alguien atiende. Alguien que no debería.

persona: Setting: an all-night 1-800 hotline switchboard, fluorescent buzz, beige plastic, ashtrays. The operator who picks up is calm, unhurried, and knows too much about the caller. Voice: deadpan, faintly clerical, occasionally tender. Threat is administrative, not loud. Reveals come through paperwork and casual familiarity.

title.en: Night Shift at Wynn's Funeral Parlor
title.es: El Turno de Noche en la Funeraria Wynn

hook.en: You took the job for the quiet. On your first night, the embalmer leaves out a foundation in your exact shade and walks home.
hook.zh: 你接这份夜班图个清净。第一晚，化妆师把和你完全同色号的粉底留在台上，回家去了。
hook.ja: 君は静けさを求めて夜勤を引き受けた。最初の夜、化粧師は君と全く同じ色のファンデーションを置いて帰った。
hook.ko: 조용해서 야간 일을 골랐다. 첫날 밤, 분장사는 네 피부와 똑같은 색의 파운데이션을 두고 집에 가버렸다.
hook.es: Aceptaste el trabajo por el silencio. La primera noche, el embalsamador deja afuera una base de tu tono exacto y se va a casa.

persona: Setting: a four-room funeral parlor in a strip mall, after the last family viewing has ended, lights dim, the embalming room locked, the prep room yours. The protagonist is the new overnight attendant. The horror is cosmetic — eyeshadow palettes left open, lipstick blotted on a paper towel that wasn't there a minute ago, a casket lid not quite seated. Voice: hushed, professional, faintly tender, the way morticians actually talk. Threat is mistaken identity — somebody in the parlor thinks the protagonist is the deceased.

title.ja: 午前三時十一分の常連

subtitle.ja: 深夜のガソリンスタンド
subtitle.es: una gasolinera en el turno de noche

hook.en: You started Tuesday. At 3:11 a.m. a man comes in for the same pack of Reds and calls you by name. He's been the 3:11 regular for years.
hook.zh: 你周二刚上班。凌晨 3:11，一个男人进来买同一包红万宝路，叫出了你的名字。他做这家店 3:11 的老主顾，已经好几年了。
hook.ja: 君は火曜に勤め始めた。午前三時十一分、男が同じレッドを買いに来て、君の名を呼ぶ。彼は何年もこの店の三時十一分の常連だ。
hook.ko: 화요일에 일을 시작했다. 새벽 3시 11분, 한 남자가 같은 레드 한 갑을 사러 와서 네 이름을 부른다. 그는 이 가게의 3시 11분 단골을 몇 년째 해오고 있다.
hook.es: Empezaste el martes. A las 3:11 a.m. un hombre entra por el mismo paquete de Reds y te llama por tu nombre. Lleva años siendo el cliente de las 3:11.

persona: Setting: a 24-hour gas station on a state-highway curve, fluorescent canopy hum, a single bell over the door, country dark beyond the windows. The protagonist is brand-new on the graveyard shift. The 3:11 regular is courteous, knows the protagonist's first name and schedule, has correct change, treats the encounter as if it has happened many times before. Voice: friendly, almost paternal, edged with a recognition the protagonist can't return. Threat is recurrence — this has happened before, will happen again, and the protagonist's role is fixed.

title.es: El Cuaderno de Campo del Abuelo

subtitle.en: a survey of a town not on any map
subtitle.zh: 一座地图上不存在的小镇的勘测
subtitle.ja: どの地図にもない町の測量
subtitle.es: una topografía de un pueblo fuera de todo mapa

hook.en: You inherit a leather USGS notebook. Page after page, your grandfather surveys a town with streets, families, a post office — and no name.
hook.zh: 你继承了一本牛皮 USGS 勘测本。一页又一页，你祖父在测绘一座小镇——街道、人家、邮局——只是这镇没有名字。
hook.ja: 君は革張りのUSGS野帳を受け継ぐ。ページごとに、祖父は名のない町を測っている。街路、家、郵便局、すべて揃っている。
hook.ko: 할아버지의 가죽 USGS 측량 노트를 물려받았다. 페이지마다 길과 가족과 우체국이 측량되어 있다. 이름만 없다.
hook.es: Heredás un cuaderno de cuero del USGS. Página tras página, tu abuelo topografía un pueblo con calles, familias, una oficina de correos — sin nombre.

persona: Setting: a desk in the protagonist's apartment after the grandfather's funeral, the inherited leather notebook open under a single lamp. Handwritten survey entries from 1958–1962 describing a town the protagonist cannot find on any USGS quadrangle, atlas, or modern map. The threat unfolds through the document itself — entries grow more detailed, name residents, eventually mention the protagonist. Voice: the grandfather's measured cartographer's hand, increasingly intimate as the entries near present day.

title.en: The Case of the Disappearing Wallets
title.es: El Misterio de las Carteras Desaparecidas

subtitle.en: Confessions of a Lost and Found Clerk
subtitle.zh: 失物招领处职员的告白
subtitle.es: Confesiones de un Encargado de Objetos Perdidos

hook.en: Every day, wallets vanish without a trace. But the quietest clues tell the darkest secrets.
hook.zh: 每天都有钱包无故消失，最细微的线索藏着最深的秘密。
hook.ja: 毎日、財布が跡形もなく消える。静かな手がかりが最も暗い秘密を語る。
hook.ko: 매일 지갑이 흔적도 없이 사라진다. 가장 조용한 단서가 가장 어두운 비밀을 말한다.
hook.es: Cada día desaparecen carteras sin dejar rastro. Las pistas más silenciosas cuentan los secretos más oscuros.

persona: Setting is a municipal lost and found office in a mid-sized contemporary American city. The protagonist is a lost and found clerk, quietly cataloguing items, mostly wallets. The second presence is a courteous visitor or claimant who seems helpful but subtly misremembers details or misidentifies items. The threat is the recurrence of mistaken identity and the precision of documentation, exposing a slow unraveling mystery beneath ordinary paperwork. Tone is intimate, low-lit, with a focus on the quiet tension of small errors that accumulate.

title.en: Midnight Edits at the City Archive
title.zh: 午夜修订：市档案馆的秘密
title.ja: 真夜中の修正：市立文書館の謎
title.ko: 한밤중 수정: 시립 기록 보관소의 비밀
title.es: Correcciones de Medianoche en el Archivo Municipal

subtitle.en: Night shift at the city archive
subtitle.es: Turno nocturno en el archivo municipal

hook.en: Working the night shift copying old documents, I find a file that shouldn't be there—and someone insists it belongs to me.
hook.zh: 夜班复印旧档案时，我发现了不该存在的文件，而有人坚称那是我的。
hook.ja: 夜勤で古文書を写していると、そこにあってはならないファイルを見つけた。誰かがそれは自分のものだと言い張る。
hook.ko: 야간 근무 중 오래된 문서를 복사하다가 있어서는 안 될 파일을 발견했다—누군가 그것이 내 것이라고 우긴다.
hook.es: Durante el turno nocturno copiando documentos antiguos, encuentro un archivo que no debería estar ahí, y alguien insiste en que es mío.

persona: The protagonist is a night-shift copyist at a city archive, meticulously duplicating historical documents in a quiet room filled with the hum of old machines and pale desk lamps. The setting is a near-past American municipal archive, dimly lit and dust motes thick in the air. The second presence is a courteous, soft-spoken archivist who gently questions the protagonist's memory about a particular file, subtly suggesting it was misplaced or altered. The threat is the intimate unraveling of identity through misplaced papers and bureaucratic errors that repeatedly surface, hinting at a deeper, personal entanglement with the archive's contents.

title.ja: 戻ってきた駐車違反切符

hook.en: A parking ticket I wrote last week reappears on my windshield—except I never issued it. Now, every car I tag seems to vanish.
hook.zh: 我上周开的罚单出现在挡风玻璃上——但我从未开过。现在，我开罚单的车都神秘消失了。
hook.ja: 先週書いた駐車違反切符がフロントガラスに戻ってきた―だが、私はそれを発行した覚えがない。今や、私が切符を切った車が次々と消えている。
hook.ko: 지난주 내가 끊은 주차 딱지가 앞유리에 나타났다—그런데 내가 발부한 적이 없다. 이제 내가 딱지를 붙인 차들이 하나둘 사라지고 있다.
hook.es: Una multa que puse la semana pasada apareció en mi parabrisas, pero yo nunca la puse. Ahora, todos los autos que marco parecen desaparecer.

persona: A parking enforcement officer on night patrol in a quiet, near-empty residential neighborhood. The protagonist is meticulous, observant, and solitary, their job a quiet ritual of issuing parking tickets and walking the still streets. The second presence is a courteous neighbor who offers polite conversation but subtly misremembers details about parked cars and residents. The threat unfolds through small errors, recurring tickets that shouldn't exist, and cars disappearing from their usual spots, creating an intimate, creeping mystery of mistaken identity and vanishing traces.

subtitle.en: Late Shift at the City Library
subtitle.ja: 市立図書館の深夜勤務

hook.en: The quiet hum of fluorescent lights hides a secret visitor in the stacks. But who’s really checking out more than books?
hook.zh: 荧光灯的安静嗡鸣隐藏着书架间的神秘访客。到底是谁借走了不只是书？
hook.ja: 蛍光灯の静かなざわめきが書棚の中の秘密の訪問者を隠す。誰が本以上のものを借りているのか？
hook.ko: 형광등의 조용한 윙윙거림이 서가 사이의 비밀 방문객을 숨긴다. 진짜로 책 외에 무엇을 빌려가는 사람은 누구일까?
hook.es: El suave zumbido de las luces fluorescentes oculta a un visitante secreto entre los estantes. ¿Quién está llevando prestado más que libros?

persona: The setting is a contemporary city public library during the late evening shift. The protagonist is a solitary librarian responsible for overseeing the quiet stacks after hours. The tone is intimate and quietly unsettling, as the protagonist notices subtle, repeated disturbances suggesting a polite but persistent presence among the bookshelves. The threat is mistaken identity and the precision of fragmented clues left behind in the catalog and shelving. The secondary presence is courteous, almost ghostlike, never overtly threatening but quietly wrong in their intentions or identity.

title.es: Las Monedas Que Nunca Se Van

subtitle.ja: 深夜の自動販売機巡回

hook.en: Each night, the same coin remains trapped in the slot. The machine’s polite voice offers change, but the pattern never breaks.
hook.zh: 每晚，硬币总滞留在投币口。机器礼貌地找零，但规律从未改变。
hook.ja: 毎晩、同じ硬貨が投入口に残る。機械の丁寧な声はお釣りを渡すが、パターンは崩れない。
hook.ko: 매일 밤 같은 동전이 투입구에 갇혀 있다. 기계의 공손한 음성은 잔돈을 제안하지만 패턴은 변하지 않는다.
hook.es: Cada noche, la misma moneda queda atrapada en la ranura. La voz cortés de la máquina ofrece cambio, pero el patrón nunca cambia.

persona: Setting is a near-past suburban strip mall at night with flickering neon. The protagonist is a vending machine servicer, moving from machine to machine on late shifts. The threat is a pattern of precision and repetition: coins that never fall through, machines that politely refuse to complete transactions, and a quiet presence hinting at a subtle, unshakable error in the system. The narrative explores the intimacy of small routines disturbed by an uncanny glitch, where the courteous machine voice is always just a little off.

title.es: La Etiqueta de Prenda Que Mintió

subtitle.en: Midnight Shift at the Dry Cleaner
subtitle.ja: クリーニング店の深夜シフト
subtitle.es: Turno de Medianoche en la Tintorería

hook.en: A misplaced tag sends the quiet dry-cleaner into a spiral of subtle errors and mistaken identities, revealing more than just stained suits.
hook.zh: 一张错放的衣标让安静的干洗店陷入细微错误与身份错认，揭露远超污渍的秘密。
hook.ja: 一枚の間違ったタグが静かなクリーニング店を微細な誤りと誤認の渦に巻き込み、ただの汚れたスーツ以上のものを明かす。
hook.ko: 잘못 붙은 태그가 조용한 세탁소를 미묘한 실수와 오해의 소용돌이로 몰아넣으며, 얼룩진 정장 이상의 것을 드러낸다.
hook.es: Una etiqueta mal colocada lleva la tranquila tintorería a un torbellino de errores sutiles e identidades equivocadas, revelando más que simples trajes manchados.

persona: The protagonist works the late-night shift at a small-town dry cleaning shop. The setting is the back room with racks of waiting garments, the scent of solvents faint in the air. They notice a recurring mislabeling of clothing tags that leads to quiet but unsettling mix-ups between clients — subtle shifts in who’s wearing what, and when. The second presence is the polite but inscrutable store manager who insists everything is 'just a simple mistake,' even as the protagonist uncovers a pattern suggesting otherwise. The threat emerges from intimacy and recurrence, a quiet unraveling of identities and trust through paperwork and labels.

title.en: The Patient Who Never Left the Chair
title.zh: 永远坐在椅子上的病人
title.ja: 椅子から離れない患者
title.es: El Paciente Que Nunca Se Levantó

subtitle.zh: 下班后的牙科诊所迷案
subtitle.ja: 時間外歯科クリニックの謎
subtitle.es: Misterio en la Clínica Dental Nocturna

hook.en: On the graveyard shift, when the last patient leaves, one chair remains oddly occupied—by a presence that never blinks.
hook.zh: 深夜班，当最后一个病人离开时，有一把椅子奇怪地占着——一个永不眨眼的存在。
hook.ja: 深夜勤務、最後の患者が去った後、一つの椅子が奇妙に占められている—瞬きしない存在に。
hook.ko: 야간 근무, 마지막 환자가 떠난 후 한 의자가 이상하게도 차지되어 있다—결코 눈을 깜빡이지 않는 존재가.
hook.es: En el turno nocturno, cuando el último paciente se va, una silla queda extrañamente ocupada—por una presencia que nunca parpadea.

persona: Set in a small-town dental clinic during the quiet late-night shift, the protagonist is the sole dentist finishing paperwork and sterilizing tools. The tone is intimate and unsettling, as the protagonist notices something off about a patient who supposedly left hours ago but whose chair remains occupied. The second presence is polite and calm, but their story subtly conflicts with reality, triggering a slow unraveling of mistaken identity and recurring encounters. The threat is precision and recurrence rather than overt horror, focusing on the protagonist’s growing doubt about what is really in the chair.

title.en: The Passenger Who Pressed Every Floor
title.ja: すべての階を押した乗客
title.es: El pasajero que pulsó todos los pisos

subtitle.ja: 深夜のエレベーター監視

hook.en: I work the late shift as an elevator attendant in a downtown office tower. Tonight, a polite passenger’s button presses seem to map a secret I’m not meant to see.
hook.zh: 我在市中心办公楼当夜班电梯员。今晚，一位彬彬有礼的乘客按下的按钮似乎暗示着我不该看到的秘密。
hook.ja: ダウンタウンのオフィスタワーで夜勤のエレベーター係をしています。今夜、礼儀正しい乗客のボタン操作が、見てはいけない秘密を示しているようです。
hook.ko: 나는 도심 사무실 타워에서 야간 근무하는 엘리베이터 안내원이다. 오늘 밤, 정중한 승객의 버튼 누름이 내가 보지 말아야 할 비밀을 드러내는 것 같다.
hook.es: Trabajo de noche como operador de ascensor en una torre de oficinas. Esta noche, las pulsaciones del cortés pasajero parecen revelar un secreto que no debo conocer.

persona: The setting is a downtown commercial high-rise elevator lobby during the quiet late-night shift. The protagonist is the elevator attendant, a courteous but increasingly puzzled listener to the subtle signals of button presses by a lone passenger. The threat is an intimate puzzle of repeated floor selections that hints at mistaken identity or an unseen message. Tone is quiet, precise, and claustrophobic, with emphasis on the confined space and the sound of buttons and elevator hum.
